description: Machine-readable CDE definitions for PanKbase single-cell / single-nucleus RNA-seq sample-level metadata. Field list is sourced from the collaborator-curated 'scRNAseq_metadata.xlsx' spreadsheet and covers single-cell/nucleus preparation (assay, library, sequencing) and post-sequencing processing steps. Sample-level identifier and donor cross-reference fields are included to make the schema usable for actual records. Donor-level fields (demographics, medical history, autoantibodies, pancreas processing) are NOT duplicated here; use donor_rrid_ref to join to the PanKbase donor CDE collection.

cdes.question_text: What is the unique identifier for this single-cell / single-nucleus RNA-seq sample?
cdes.notes: Free-text string. Must be unique within a study; global uniqueness is recommended. Origin: identifier CDE (not in the collaborator spreadsheet) added so that the schema can be used to harmonize actual records.

cdes.question_text: What is the RRID of the donor from whom this sample was derived?

cross_reference.description: Links this scRNA-seq sample to the donor record defined in the PanKbase Donor Metadata CDE collection. Value domain (RRID pattern) and semantics are governed by PKB_D_001.

cdes.notes: Accepts a canonical 'RRID:SAMN########' value, a bare BioSample accession ('SAMN########'), or a consortium-native donor ID (e.g., 'HPAP-019', 'HP-21070-01') when no RRID has been assigned. Origin: identifier CDE (not in the collaborator spreadsheet) added so the schema can join to the donor CDE collection.

cdes.question_text: At what resolution was the assay performed?
cdes.notes: Whether the assay captured intact cells (e.g., scRNA-seq) or isolated nuclei (e.g., snRNA-seq). Origin: collaborator spreadsheet (scRNAseq_metadata.xlsx) — 'Assay resolution'.

cdes.question_text: Which molecular modality (or modalities) does this assay measure?
cdes.notes: Multiome assays (e.g., 10x Multiome ATAC + Gene Expression) should be recorded as 'multiome'. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Modality'.

cdes.question_text: Which single-cell assay platform was used?
cdes.notes: Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Assay platform'.

cdes.question_text: Which reagent / library-preparation kit was used?
cdes.notes: Free text identifying the kit and chemistry version where available. Example: 'Chromium Next GEM Single Cell Multiome ATAC + Gene Expression', '10X-Chromium-GEX-3p-v2', '10X-Chromium-GEX-3p-v3'. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Reagen kit' (note: spelled 'Reagen' in the source; canonicalized to 'reagent_kit').

cdes.question_text: What treatment protocol was applied to the cells/nuclei prior to capture?
cdes.notes: Free-text description of the treatment protocol applied prior to capture. May be 'None' / 'Untreated' when no treatment was applied. Example: 'CMRL (10% FBS, 1% Glutamax) supplemented with 0.025% DMSO, 250 nM thapsigargin, or 25 U/mL IL1β + 1000 U/mL IFNγ for 24 h at 37°C, 5% CO2.' Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Treatment protocol'.

cdes.question_text: What growth / culture protocol was used for the source cells/tissue?
cdes.notes: Free-text description of how the source biospecimen (e.g., islets) was procured and maintained prior to dissociation. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Growth protocol'.

cdes.question_text: What molecule was extracted from the cells/nuclei?
cdes.notes: Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Extracted molecule'.

cdes.question_text: What protocol was used to extract and capture the cells/nuclei?
cdes.notes: Free-text description of cell/nucleus dissociation, staining, hashing, and droplet/well loading. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Extraction protocol'.

cdes.question_text: What library-construction protocol (text or URL) was followed?
cdes.notes: Free text. URLs are encouraged when a public protocol document exists (e.g., https://hpap.pmacs.upenn.edu/explore/workflow/islet-molecular-phenotyping-studies?protocol=4). Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Library construction protocol'.

cdes.question_text: Which library selection strategy was used?
cdes.notes: Vocabulary follows SRA / ENA conventions. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Library selection'.

cdes.question_text: Was the library sequenced as paired-end or single-end?
cdes.notes: Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Sequencing run type'.

cdes.question_text: Which sequencing instrument was used?
cdes.notes: Free text, ideally manufacturer + model. Example: 'Illumina NovaSeq 6000', 'Illumina HiSeq 4000'. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Sequencing instrument'.

cdes.question_text: What was the planned read-length configuration of the sequencing run?
cdes.notes: Format '<R1>x<R2>' for paired-end (e.g., '101x101'), or a single integer for single-end (e.g., '75'). Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Read length configuration'.

cdes.question_text: What were the read lengths in the demultiplexed FASTQ files (R1/R2)?
cdes.notes: The actual read lengths in delivered FASTQs, excluding index reads (i7/i5). Format '<R1>x<R2>' (e.g., '26x98'). Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Demultiplexed read lengths'.

cdes.question_text: What was the targeted (loaded) cell/nucleus count for this library?
cdes.notes: Targeted/planned cell number loaded into the assay; not the post-QC recovered count. Example: 5000. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Number of target cells'.

cdes.question_text: Provide a high-level description of the data-processing workflow.
cdes.notes: Free text covering BCL→FASTQ conversion, alignment, count-matrix generation, demultiplexing, hashtag processing, etc. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Processing workflow overview'.

cdes.question_text: Where can the code or pipeline definition for the processing workflow be found?
cdes.notes: Free text; URL preferred (GitHub repo, Snakemake/Nextflow definition, DOI). Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Processing workflow code'.

cdes.question_text: Which reference genome build was used for alignment?
cdes.notes: Free text. Example: 'GRCh38', 'GRCh38.93', 'GRCh38-2020-A', 'refdata-gex-GRCh38-2020-A'. Annotation bundle SHOULD be included when known. Origin: collaborator spreadsheet — 'Genome build'.

version_history.changes: Initial draft. 20 CDEs derived from metadata_for_DEG.rds (per-cell sequencing/CellBender/mitochondrial QC) plus 1 cross-reference to the donor CDE collection and 6 inferred fields commonly expected in scRNA-seq metadata. Retired in v0.2 in favor of a collaborator-curated field list.

Replaced the v0.1 field set with 20 CDEs sourced from the collaborator-curated 'scRNAseq_metadata.xlsx' spreadsheet (2 identifier CDEs + 15 Single Cell/Nucleus Preparation + 3 Processing Steps). Categories are now 'Sample Identification', 'Single Cell/Nucleus Preparation', and 'Processing Steps'. Per-cell QC fields (mean_umi_count_per_cell, CellBender metrics, mean_pct_mitochondrial_reads, etc.) and the RDS-derived inferred fields are removed. The previously-shipped per-cell QC artifacts (data/metadata_for_DEG.rds mapping + harmonized output) no longer match this schema; the RDS data file is retained in the repo as a historical reference.
